response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong testing discipline, polyglot range (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, Java, C#), and a notable C++ stint at National Instruments — but that C++ work was a brief ~6-month engagement over a decade ago. Recent experience centers on web/backend platforms (TypeScript, Node, MongoDB, AWS), with no demonstrated modern C++ or Rust work, no distributed routing/geospatial tiling pipeline experience, and no navigation guidance background. While GISCollective shows genuine geospatial interest, the core technical requirements for a routing-tiles role are not met.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain interest via long-running GISCollective open-source platform
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, low-latency systems at Sociomantic
Polyglot adaptability and willingness to work across the full stack
concerns: No modern C++ experience — only a short stint 11 years ago at National Instruments
No Rust experience listed anywhere in resume or skills
No distributed-systems or routing/tiling pipeline production work; recent focus is TypeScript/Node web platforms
